{"query":"Red Lobster August 2024 sale 23 restaurants bankruptcy court buyers","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1710,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://abcnews.com/GMA/Food/judge-clears-red-lobster-exit-bankruptcy-sale-new/story?id=113456238","title":"Judge clears Red Lobster to exit from bankruptcy with sale to new owner - ABC News","content":"There may be smooth sailing ahead for Red Lobster after the popular seafood restaurant chain received court approval for its Chapter 11 plan to sell the company to a new firm.
Red Lobster Management LLC announced in a press release Thursday that the restaurant chain will be acquired by RL Investor Holdings LLC -- an entity "created by funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group LLC" with co-investors TCW Private Credit and Blue Torch Capital -- and will operate as an independent company.
According to documents obtained by ABC News, Red Lobster's voluntary Chapter 11 plan, which was filed on May 19, 2024, in the U.S.
"With our new backers, we have a comprehensive and long-term investment plan -- including a commitment of more than $60 million in new funding -- that will help to reinvigorate the iconic brand while keeping the best of its history."
Tibus added in the statement that Red Lobster will emerge "stronger financially and operationally" with "new backers who are resolutely focused on investment and growth."
Earlier this year, Red Lobster closed at least 99 restaurants, however it still operates 544 locations across 44 states and in four Canadian Provinces."},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/red-lobster-moves-closer-bankruptcy-sale-lenders-2024-07-23/","title":"Red Lobster moves closer to bankruptcy sale to lenders | Reuters","content":"NEW YORK, July 23 (Reuters) - Red Lobster is moving closer to a bankruptcy sale that would put lenders, including Fortress Investment Group, in charge of the company after no other bidders stepped up with an offer to repay the struggling U.S. restaurant chain's debt, according to court documents.
Red Lobster filed for bankruptcy in Florida in May with about $300 million in debt and a plan to close some restaurants and sell itself to its lenders or a higher bidder."},{"url":"https://people.com/red-lobster-to-close-23-more-restaurants-filing-bankruptcy-8701577","title":"Red Lobster to Close 23 More Restaurants After Filing for Bankruptcy in May","content":"According to a court filing obtained by PEOPLE, 23 locations are set to close by Saturday, Aug.
The news comes after the seafood chain filed for voluntary Chapter 11 bankruptcy in Florida back in May.
Prior to filing for bankruptcy, it was reported that the company’s signature endless shrimp deal caused the chain to lose $11 million."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2024/05/20/red-lobster-bankruptcy.html","title":"Red Lobster files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection","content":"Red Lobster has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, continuing the process to shrink its footprint and find a buyer, the company said in a statement.
Orlando, Florida-based Red Lobster has assets between $1 billion and $10 billion and estimated liabilities of $1 billion to $10 billion, according to the bankruptcy filing.
Nearly two decades later, Darden sold Red Lobster to Golden Gate.
In 2020, Thai Union, members of Red Lobster management and investors using the alias Seafood Alliance bought out Golden Gate's remaining stake in the chain.
According to a court filing, the ill-conceived promotion's actual aim may have been more about boosting Thai Union's own sales.
Correction: Performance Food Group is claiming Red Lobster owes it $24.4 million."}]}
